title: Game creation system

Please [improve it](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Game_creation_system&action=edit) by [verifying](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability") the claims made and adding [inline citations](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources#Inline_citations "Wikipedia:Citing sources"). Statements consisting only of original research should be removed. *(March 2016)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |  |  |  | | --- | --- | |  | This article may **require [cleanup](/wiki/Wikipedia:Cleanup "Wikipedia:Cleanup")** to meet Wikipedia's [quality standards](/wiki/Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style "Wikipedia:Manual of Style"). The specific problem is: **poor organization of ideas.** Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Game_creation_system "Special:EditPage/Game creation system") if you can. *(March 2016)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |  *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

(**GCS**) is a [game engine](/wiki/Game_engine "Game engine") that is targeted towards the consumer, for the purpose of creating a [user-derived](/wiki/User-generated_content "User-generated content") [video game](/wiki/Video_game "Video game"). It contains a set of specialized design tools to make a video game, and sometimes also a light [scripting language](/wiki/Scripting_language "Scripting language").

*[Adventure Master](/wiki/Adventure_Master "Adventure Master")*, [World Builder](/wiki/World_Builder "World Builder"), [Adventure Game Studio](/wiki/Adventure_Game_Studio "Adventure Game Studio"), [Twine](/wiki/Twine_(software) "Twine (software)"), [Wintermute Engine](/wiki/Wintermute_Engine "Wintermute Engine"), *SLUDGE*[[8]](#cite_note-8)

[3D Game Creation System](/wiki/Pie_in_the_Sky_(game_engine) "Pie in the Sky (game engine)"), [FPS Creator](/wiki/The_Game_Creators "The Game Creators"), *Silent Walk FPS Creator*,[[9]](#cite_note-9) *Raycasting Game Maker*,[[10]](#cite_note-10) *Easy FPS Editor*[[11]](#cite_note-11)

[RPG Maker](/wiki/RPG_Maker "RPG Maker"), [Official Hamster Republic Role Playing Game Construction Engine](/wiki/Official_Hamster_Republic_Role_Playing_Game_Construction_Engine "Official Hamster Republic Role Playing Game Construction Engine"), *EasyRPG*,[[13]](#cite_note-13) *Solarus*,[[14]](#cite_note-14) *Sphere*[[15]](#cite_note-15)

*[Forgotten Realms: Unlimited Adventures](/wiki/Forgotten_Realms:_Unlimited_Adventures "Forgotten Realms: Unlimited Adventures")*, *[The Bard's Tale Construction Set](/wiki/The_Bard%27s_Tale_Construction_Set "The Bard's Tale Construction Set")*

*[LittleBigPlanet](/wiki/LittleBigPlanet "LittleBigPlanet")*, *[Super Mario Maker](/wiki/Super_Mario_Maker "Super Mario Maker")*, *PlataGO!*, *[Geometry Dash](/wiki/Geometry_Dash "Geometry Dash")*[[16]](#cite_note-16)

[*Dreams*](/wiki/Dreams_(video_game) "Dreams (video game)"), [*Nintendo Labo Toy-Con 04: VR Kit*](/wiki/Nintendo_Labo#VR_Kit "Nintendo Labo"), *[Game Builder Garage](/wiki/Game_Builder_Garage "Game Builder Garage")*, *[Fancade](/wiki/Fancade "Fancade")*

insert credit, ["From Shooter to Shooter: The Rise of cly5m"](https://insertcredit.com/2011/06/30/from-shooter-to-shooter-the-rise-of-cly5m/) [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20120119215221/http://insertcredit.com/2011/06/30/from-shooter-to-shooter-the-rise-of-cly5m/) 2012-01-19 at the [Wayback Machine](/wiki/Wayback_Machine "Wayback Machine")

["War Game Construction Kit"](https://web.archive.org/web/20130728111753/http://retropc.net/fm-7/museum/softhouse/ascii/000701300.html). *[Oh!FM](https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oh!FM "ja:Oh!FM")*. Archived from the original on 28 July 2013. Retrieved 3 September 2012.`{{cite web}}`: CS1 maint: bot: original URL status unknown ([link](/wiki/Category:CS1_maint:_bot:_original_URL_status_unknown "Category:CS1 maint: bot: original URL status unknown"))

["Thunder Force Construction"](https://web.archive.org/web/20130728113345/http://retropc.net/fm-7/museum/softhouse/tecnosoft/330602301.html). *[Oh!FM](https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oh!FM "jp:Oh!FM")*. Archived from the original on 28 July 2013. Retrieved 1 September 201

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Special software used to make video games

|  |  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  | **This article has multiple issues.** Please help **[improve it](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Game_creation_system "Special:EditPage/Game creation system")** or discuss these issues on the **[talk page](/wiki/Talk:Game_creation_system "Talk:Game creation system")**. *([Learn how and when to remove these messages](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |  |  | | --- | --- | |  | This article **may contain [original research](/wiki/Wikipedia:No_original_research "Wikipedia:No original research")**. Please [improve it](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Game_creation_system&action=edit) by [verifying](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability") the claims made and adding [inline citations](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citing_sources#Inline_citations "Wikipedia:Citing sources"). Statements consisting only of original research should be removed. *(March 2016)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |  |  |  | | --- | --- | |  | This article may **require [cleanup](/wiki/Wikipedia:Cleanup "Wikipedia:Cleanup")** to meet Wikipedia's [quality standards](/wiki/Wikipedia:Manual_of_Style "Wikipedia:Manual of Style"). The specific problem is: **poor organization of ideas.** Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Game_creation_system "Special:EditPage/Game creation system") if you can. *(March 2016)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |  *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

A **game creation system** (**GCS**) is a [game engine](/wiki/Game_engine "Game engine") that is targeted towards the consumer, for the purpose of creating a [user-derived](/wiki/User-generated_content "User-generated content") [video game](/wiki/Video_game "Video game"). It contains a set of specialized design tools to make a video game, and sometimes also a light [scripting language](/wiki/Scripting_language "Scripting language").

Unlike developer-oriented game engines, game creation systems allow an easy entry point for novice or hobbyist game designers, with often little to no coding required for simple behaviors. Although initially stigmatized, all-in-one game creation systems have gained some legitimacy with the role of [Unity](/wiki/Unity_(game_engine) "Unity (game engine)"), [Pixel Game Maker MV](/wiki/Pixel_Game_Maker_MV "Pixel Game Maker MV"), and [GameMaker](/wiki/GameMaker "GameMaker") in the growth of the indie game development community.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Currently the [Independent Games Festival](/wiki/Independent_Games_Festival "Independent Games Festival") recognizes games produced with similar platforms.

Early game creation systems such as [Broderbund](/wiki/Broderbund "Broderbund")'s *[The Arcade Machine](/wiki/The_Arcade_Machine "The Arcade Machine")* (1982), *[Pinball Construction Set](/wiki/Pinball_Construction_Set "Pinball Construction Set")* (1983), [ASCII](/wiki/ASCII_(company) "ASCII (company)")'s *War Game Construction Kit* (1983),[[2]](#cite_note-2) *[Thunder Force Construction](/wiki/Thunder_Force_(series) "Thunder Force (series)")* (1984),[[3]](#cite_note-3) *[Adventure Construction Set](/wiki/Adventure_Construction_Set "Adventure Construction Set")* (1984), *[Garry Kitchen's GameMaker](/wiki/Garry_Kitchen%27s_GameMaker "Garry Kitchen's GameMaker")* (1985), *[Wargame Construction Set](/wiki/Wargame_Construction_Set "Wargame Construction Set")* (1986), *[Shoot-'Em-Up Construction Kit](/wiki/Shoot-%27Em-Up_Construction_Kit "Shoot-'Em-Up Construction Kit")* (1987), *[Mamirin / Dungeon Manjirou](/wiki/RPG_Maker "RPG Maker")* (1988), and *[Arcade Game Construction Kit](/wiki/Arcade_Game_Construction_Kit "Arcade Game Construction Kit")* (1988) appeared in the 1980s on [home computers](/wiki/Home_computer "Home computer"). *[3D Construction Kit](/wiki/3D_Construction_Kit "3D Construction Kit")* was released on the [ZX Spectrum](/wiki/ZX_Spectrum "ZX Spectrum") in 1991, and contained a full polygon-based world creation tool. Most of these early design frameworks are specific to one or another genre.

In the 1990s, game creation systems for the [IBM PC](/wiki/IBM_PC "IBM PC") expanded to the more general and the more specific. Whereas frameworks like RSD [Game-Maker](/wiki/Game-Maker "Game-Maker") and [Klik & Play](/wiki/Klik_%26_Play "Klik & Play") attempted to accommodate any genre, communities grew around games like *[ZZT](/wiki/ZZT "ZZT")* (later *MegaZeux*[[4]](#cite_note-4)) that permitted extensive user modification that they essentially became de facto game creation systems. Pie in the Sky Software created a full on [2.5D](/wiki/2.5D "2.5D") [first-person shooter](/wiki/First-person_shooter "First-person shooter") creator out of [an engine](/wiki/Pie_in_the_Sky_(game_engine) "Pie in the Sky (game engine)") they previously used internally, which sold in three total versions until 2003; [3D GameStudio](/wiki/3D_GameStudio "3D GameStudio") and products by [The Game Creators](/wiki/The_Game_Creators "The Game Creators") have targeted similar creators.

In the mid-2000s, with the growth of the [World Wide Web](/wiki/World_Wide_Web "World Wide Web") and [social networking](/wiki/Social_networking "Social networking"), programs like [BlitzBasic](/wiki/BlitzBasic "BlitzBasic") and [Multimedia Fusion](/wiki/Multimedia_Fusion "Multimedia Fusion") supports the rise of interest both in [indie games](/wiki/Indie_games "Indie games") and in canned game design software.[[5]](#cite_note-5) Whereas earlier game creation systems tend to be on the side of user friendly interfaces,[[6]](#cite_note-GamaInt-6) 21st-century systems often have extensive scripting languages that can account for higher-skilled users.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] Other general purpose game creation systems include [Construct](/wiki/Construct_(software) "Construct (software)"), [Clickteam](/wiki/Clickteam "Clickteam"), [Buildbox](/wiki/Buildbox "Buildbox"), [Game Editor](/wiki/Game_Editor "Game Editor"), [GameSalad](/wiki/GameSalad "GameSalad"), [GDevelop](/wiki/GDevelop "GDevelop") and [Stencyl](/wiki/Stencyl "Stencyl").

## Features

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Game_creation_system&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Features")]

### Tools

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Game_creation_system&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Tools")]

Several game creation systems include some of the following tools:

* [Integrated development environment](/wiki/Integrated_development_environment "Integrated development environment"): for managing projects and resources
* [Command-line interface](/wiki/Command-line_interface "Command-line interface"): for compiling and debugging games
* Sprite editor: for the editing of animated images commonly referred to as [sprites](/wiki/Sprite_(computer_graphics) "Sprite (computer graphics)")
* Model editor: for [3D modeling](/wiki/3D_modeling "3D modeling") purposes
* Map/Scene editor: generally used for object and tile placement

### Scripting

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Game_creation_system&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Scripting")]

The rise of game creation systems also saw a need for free form [scripting languages](/wiki/Scripting_languages "Scripting languages") with general purpose use. Some packages, such as Conitec's [Gamestudio](/wiki/3D_GameStudio "3D GameStudio"), include a more comprehensive scripting language under the surface to allow users more leeway in defining their games' behavior.

## Usage

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Game_creation_system&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Usage")]

While most of the mainstream and popular game creation systems may be general-purpose, several exist solely for specific genres.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

* **Adventure games:** *[Adventure Master](/wiki/Adventure_Master "Adventure Master")*, [World Builder](/wiki/World_Builder "World Builder"), [Adventure Game Studio](/wiki/Adventure_Game_Studio "Adventure Game Studio"), [Twine](/wiki/Twine_(software) "Twine (software)"), [Wintermute Engine](/wiki/Wintermute_Engine "Wintermute Engine"), *SLUDGE*[[8]](#cite_note-8)
* **First-person shooters:** [3D Game Creation System](/wiki/Pie_in_the_Sky_(game_engine) "Pie in the Sky (game engine)"), [FPS Creator](/wiki/The_Game_Creators "The Game Creators"), *Silent Walk FPS Creator*,[[9]](#cite_note-9) *Raycasting Game Maker*,[[10]](#cite_note-10) *Easy FPS Editor*[[11]](#cite_note-11)
* **Fighting games**: *[Fighter Maker](/wiki/Fighter_Maker "Fighter Maker")*, [Mugen](/wiki/Mugen_(game_engine) "Mugen (game engine)"), *IKEMEN Go*[[12]](#cite_note-12)
* **[Role-playing games](/wiki/Role-playing_game_creation_software "Role-playing game creation software")**: [RPG Maker](/wiki/RPG_Maker "RPG Maker"), [Official Hamster Republic Role Playing Game Construction Engine](/wiki/Official_Hamster_Republic_Role_Playing_Game_Construction_Engine "Official Hamster Republic Role Playing Game Construction Engine"), *EasyRPG*,[[13]](#cite_note-13) *Solarus*,[[14]](#cite_note-14) *Sphere*[[15]](#cite_note-15)
* **Dungeon crawlers**: *[Forgotten Realms: Unlimited Adventures](/wiki/Forgotten_Realms:_Unlimited_Adventures "Forgotten Realms: Unlimited Adventures")*, *[The Bard's Tale Construction Set](/wiki/The_Bard%27s_Tale_Construction_Set "The Bard's Tale Construction Set")*
* **Space combat game**: *[Wing Commander Academy](/wiki/Wing_Commander_Academy_(video_game) "Wing Commander Academy (video game)")*
* **Visual novels**: [Ren'Py](/wiki/Ren%27Py "Ren'Py")
* **Massively multiplayer online games**: [Roblox](/wiki/Roblox "Roblox"), *[Core](/wiki/Core_(video_game) "Core (video game)")*, *[Rec Room](/wiki/Rec_Room_(video_game) "Rec Room (video game)")*
* **Platform games**: *[LittleBigPlanet](/wiki/LittleBigPlanet "LittleBigPlanet")*, *[Super Mario Maker](/wiki/Super_Mario_Maker "Super Mario Maker")*, *PlataGO!*, *[Geometry Dash](/wiki/Geometry_Dash "Geometry Dash")*[[16]](#cite_note-16)
* **General-purpose**: [*Dreams*](/wiki/Dreams_(video_game) "Dreams (video game)"), [*Nintendo Labo Toy-Con 04: VR Kit*](/wiki/Nintendo_Labo#VR_Kit "Nintendo Labo"), *[Game Builder Garage](/wiki/Game_Builder_Garage "Game Builder Garage")*, *[Fancade](/wiki/Fancade "Fancade")*

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Game_creation_system&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: See also")]

* [Game engine](/wiki/Game_engine "Game engine")
* [Level editor](/wiki/Level_editor "Level editor")

## References

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Game_creation_system&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: References")]

Archived from the original on 28 July 2013. Retrieved 3 September 2012.`{{cite web}}`: CS1 maint: bot: original URL status unknown ([link](/wiki/Category:CS1_maint:_bot:_original_URL_status_unknown "Category:CS1 maint: bot: original URL status unknown"))

Archived from the original on 28 July 2013. Retrieved 1 September 2012.`{{cite web}}`: CS1 maint: bot: original URL status unknown ([link](/wiki/Category:CS1_maint:_bot:_original_URL_status_unknown "Category:CS1 maint: bot: original URL status unknown"))
4. **[^](#cite_ref-4)** Wood, Evelyn (2016-10-06). ["Succeeding MegaZeux"](https://eev.ee/blog/2016/10/06/succeeding-megazeux/). *fuzzy notepad*. Retrieved 2023-02-14.
